story.iWant: my uploaded preset file to be loaded into memory
story.soThat: it can be processed by the conversion engine
story.tasks:
### Tasks & Subtasks
**Task 1: Implement FileReader API Integration** - Subtask 1.1: Create readFileAsArrayBuffer() promise wrapper - Subtask 1.2: Add loading state UI during file read - Subtask 1.3: Handle FileReader errors with try/catch
**Task 2: Data Conversion Pipeline** - Subtask 2.1: Convert ArrayBuffer to Uint8Array - Subtask 2.2: Store file data in application state (currentFileData) - Subtask 2.3: Export getCurrentFileData() for other modules
**Task 3: File Metadata Display** - Subtask 3.1: Extract file name, size, and extension - Subtask 3.2: Format file size (bytes → KB/MB) - Subtask 3.3: Update #fileInfo div with metadata - Subtask 3.4: Implement XSS protection via escapeHtml()
**Task 4: File Validation** - Subtask 4.1: Validate file size (<10MB limit) - Subtask 4.2: Check for empty files (0 bytes) - Subtask 4.3: Display appropriate error messages
**Task 5: Memory Management** - Subtask 5.1: Clear previous file data on new upload - Subtask 5.2: Implement clearFileData() function - Subtask 5.3: Dispatch 'fileLoaded' custom event
**Task 6: Integration with Story 2-1** - Subtask 6.1: Update main.js to import handleFile - Subtask 6.2: Pass handleFile callback to initializeDropZone - Subtask 6.3: Add event listener for 'fileLoaded' event

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: File Reading via FileReader API - Accept File object from drag-drop or file picker (from Story 2-1) - Use FileReader.readAsArrayBuffer() to read file contents - Handle asynchronous file reading with Promises - Display loading indicator while reading (for large files) **Test:** Upload 50KB XMP file → reads in <100ms | Upload 5MB file → shows loading indicator, completes successfully
### AC-2: Data Conversion (File → Uint8Array) - Convert ArrayBuffer to Uint8Array - Store in application state for subsequent operations - Uint8Array accessible to format detection (Story 2-3) and conversion (Story 2-6) **Test:** Upload file → Uint8Array created | Verify: fileData instanceof Uint8Array === true | Verify: fileData.length matches original file size
### AC-3: File Metadata Display - Extract and display: file name, file size (formatted), file extension - Update UI with file info (show hidden #fileInfo div from Story 2-1) - Clear previous file info when new file uploaded **Test:** Upload Classic Chrome.xmp (15KB) → display "File: Classic Chrome.xmp (15.23 KB)"
### AC-4: File Size Validation - Accept files up to 10MB - Reject files >10MB with error: "File too large. Maximum size: 10MB" **Test:** Upload 5MB file → accepted | Upload 15MB file → error message
### AC-5: Error Handling - File read failure → error: "Failed to read file. Please try again." - Corrupted file (0 bytes) → error: "File appears to be empty or corrupted" - User cancels file picker → no error, return to default state
### AC-6: Memory Management - Clear previous file data when new file uploaded (prevent memory leak) - Release object URLs when no longer needed - Handle rapid file uploads (debounce if needed) **Test:** Upload 10 files in succession → memory usage doesn't grow unbounded

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Converts File object to Uint8Array for WASM. Uses FileReader.readAsArrayBuffer() to read file contents. Handles files up to 10MB. Displays file name and size. Complexity: Simple (1 day).

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: JavaScript FileReader reads file as ArrayBuffer → Convert ArrayBuffer → Uint8Array → Call detectFormat(uint8array). All data stays in browser memory (no persistence).

doc.title: Technical Specification: Epic 2 - Web Interface
doc.snippet: Decision: Vanilla JavaScript (zero dependencies). Rationale: Epic 2 UI is simple, zero framework overhead, faster initial load, target <100KB total JS.

doc.title: Story 2-1: HTML Drag-Drop UI
doc.snippet: Story 2-2 requires Story 2-1 complete (HTML structure and drag-drop events). Story 2-2 blocks: Story 2-3 (Format Detection), 2-4 (Parameter Preview), 2-6 (WASM Conversion) - all need Uint8Array.

doc.title: Story 2-1: HTML Drag-Drop UI
doc.snippet: initializeDropZone accepts callback function handleFile. File validation done in Story 2-1, actual file reading implemented in Story 2-2. Uses custom 'fileLoaded' event for cross-module communication.

artifact.reason: Existing file validation logic that needs to be expanded with FileReader integration, Uint8Array conversion, and file metadata display per Story 2-2
Sets up event handlers for drag-drop and file picker. Story 2-2 extends handleFile callback with actual file reading functionality
Existing error display functions to reuse for file reading errors (AC-5: Error Handling)
Hidden div that Story 2-2 will populate with file metadata (name, size, extension) per AC-3
Story 2-2 updates to listen for 'fileLoaded' custom event and pass handleFile callback to initializeDropZone
WASM initialization and event dispatching pattern. Story 2-2 follows similar pattern with 'fileLoaded' event for cross-module communication

javascript.note: Zero npm dependencies - vanilla JavaScript only

constraints.constraint: Must use FileReader API (not File.arrayBuffer()) for browser compatibility with older Safari versions
File size limit: 10MB maximum (typical preset files are <100KB)
Must convert to Uint8Array format (required by WASM Go []byte mapping)
XSS prevention: All user-controlled text (file names) must be escaped before DOM insertion using escapeHtml()
Memory management: Must clear previous file data on new upload to prevent memory leaks
Privacy: All processing client-side only, no network requests (except initial WASM load)
ES6 modules: Use import/export syntax, no bundler for MVP
Browser support: Chrome/Firefox/Safari latest 2 versions, no IE11
Vanilla JavaScript: Zero framework dependencies per Tech Spec Decision 1
Architecture: Separation of concerns - file-handler.js handles I/O, main.js coordinates modules

interface.name: handleFile(file)
interface.description: Main entry point for file processing. Validates, reads, converts to Uint8Array, dispatches fileLoaded event

interface.name: readFileAsArrayBuffer(file)
interface.description: Promise wrapper around FileReader.readAsArrayBuffer(). Returns ArrayBuffer on success, rejects on error

interface.name: getCurrentFileData()
interface.description: Returns currently loaded file data for use by Story 2-3 (format detection) and 2-6 (conversion)

interface.name: getCurrentFileName()
interface.description: Returns current file name for display and download filename generation (Story 2-7)

interface.name: fileLoaded custom event
interface.description: Dispatched when file successfully loaded. Listened to by main.js to notify other modules (Stories 2-3, 2-4, 2-6)

interface.name: FileReader API
interface.description: Asynchronous file reading API. Used for compatibility and future progress event support

tests.standards: Epic 2 uses manual testing approach per Tech Spec. No automated E2E tests until Epic 6. Testing standards: browser DevTools for verification, console logging for debugging, manual cross-browser testing in Chrome/Firefox/Safari latest 2 versions. Performance testing via performance.now() benchmarks. Memory testing via DevTools Memory profiler (heap snapshots).

locations.location: Manual testing per Story acceptance criteria
Browser DevTools Console for performance logging
Browser DevTools Network tab for privacy verification (zero requests)
Browser DevTools Memory profiler for leak detection

test.description: Upload 50KB XMP file, measure read time with performance.now(), verify <100ms
Upload 5MB file, verify loading indicator displays, verify completion and console log "File loaded: [size] bytes"
Upload file, verify fileData instanceof Uint8Array === true via console
Verify fileData.length matches original file.size via console comparison
Upload Classic Chrome.xmp (15KB), verify #fileInfo div displays "File: Classic Chrome.xmp (15.23 KB)"
Upload second file without refresh, verify previous file info cleared and new info displayed
Create 5MB test file (dd if=/dev/zero of=test.xmp bs=1M count=5), upload, verify accepted
Create 15MB test file, upload, verify error "File too large. Maximum size: 10MB"
Create empty file (touch empty.xmp), upload, verify error "File appears to be empty or corrupted"
Open file picker, click Cancel, verify no error message and UI remains in default state
Upload 10 files in succession, take heap snapshots in DevTools Memory profiler, verify memory usage stable and only one Uint8Array retained
